paper_title: Opportunity analysis of agriculture products in Sri Lanka: Measuring markets and feasibility
research_answer: Sri Lanka's primary Cinnamon export destination is Mexico, which is a notable mismatch with broader global market trends. Globally, the EU and USA together account for over 60% of world imports across selected agricultural sectors, and Cinnamon is identified as one of the fastest-growing products in the EU market specifically. Meanwhile, Mexico is not among the five major market blocs (EU, USA, Middle East, East Asia, India) that represent 80.7% of world agricultural trade. This suggests Sri Lanka may be underexploiting the high-growth EU and US markets for Cinnamon, where demand momentum is strongest.
supporting_quotes: Mango, Vanilla, Ginger, Turmeric, and Cinnamon have the highest growth rates in the EU market.
Shrimp, Pepper, Mango, Vanilla, Ginger & Cinnamon have the highest growth rates
EU & USA: Almost all the products have their largest markets in the EU & USA
